name: Rice
description: Rice (aka Oryza sativa), culturally and economically important grass and its edible starchy cereal grain. Roughly one-half of the world population is wholly dependent upon it as a staple food.

diseases.name: Bacterial blight
diseases.description: A devastating bacterial disease characterized by wilting of young leaves and grayish-yellow stripes on leaf margins.
symptoms: Water-soaked to yellowish stripes on leaf margins that enlarge and turn white to grayish-yellow
Wilting of young leaves (kresek phase in seedlings)
Milky or opaque bacterial ooze visible on cut leaf ends
treatment: Use resistant varieties (e.g. BRRI dhan28, IR64)
Apply copper oxychloride or streptomycin sulfate spray at early infection
Drain fields and avoid excess nitrogen fertilizer
Remove and destroy infected plant debris

diseases.name: Bacterial leaf streak
diseases.description: A bacterial disease causing translucent interveinal streaks that later turn brown.
symptoms: Interveinal, water-soaked, translucent streaks that turn brown and narrow
Lesions bordered by leaf veins and sometimes surrounded by a yellow halo
Bacterial ooze drying into beads on the leaf surface
treatment: Reduce plant density for better air circulation

diseases.name: Foot rot
diseases.description: A basal stem rot with foul smell that causes rapid collapse of affected tillers.
symptoms: Dark, water-soaked rotting at the stem base
Characteristic foul odor
Rotted tillers pull out easily and plants collapse
treatment: Treat seeds with hot water or bleach solution before planting
Remove and burn infected plants immediately
Focus on prevention since curative control is limited

diseases.name: Grain rot
diseases.description: A bacterial panicle disease that causes floret rot, sterility, and shriveled grains.
symptoms: Rotting of florets at or shortly after heading
Glumes turn pale brown to straw-colored
Grains fail to develop or remain empty and shriveled
treatment: Treat seeds with oxolinic acid or flumequine-based bactericides
Apply propiconazole or iprodione at heading
Avoid dense planting and excess nitrogen
Improve field aeration and rotate crops

diseases.name: Pecky rice (kernel spotting)
diseases.pathogen: Bacterial complex with insect-associated grain spotting
diseases.description: Grain spotting disorder where bacterial damage contributes to dark discolored kernels.
symptoms: Small dark discolored spots on pericarp and endosperm
Kernels appear chalky, discolored, or necrotic
Damage often becomes obvious after milling
treatment: Control stink bug and other insect vectors
Apply fungicides during grain fill to reduce fungal contribution

diseases.name: Sheath brown rot
diseases.description: A bacterial sheath disease causing panicle sterility and grain discoloration.
symptoms: Brown water-soaked lesions on leaf sheaths around nodes
Rotting of sheath enclosing the young panicle
Partial or complete panicle sterility with discolored grains
treatment: Treat seeds with copper compounds or streptomycin
Avoid cold water irrigation during booting
Plant tolerant varieties and reduce density
Use balanced fertilization and avoid excess nitrogen

diseases.name: Aggregate sheath spot
diseases.description: A sheath disease with elongated lesions that can aggregate and coalesce.
symptoms: Oval to elongated lesions on sheaths with straw-colored centers and brown borders
Lesions aggregate and coalesce
Symptoms resemble sheath blight but are more elongated
treatment: Apply validamycin, hexaconazole, or propiconazole early

diseases.name: Black horse riding
diseases.description: A Curvularia disease causing dark spotting on leaves and glumes with grain quality loss.
symptoms: Small brown to black spots on leaves and glumes
Spots may have yellow halos
Grain discoloration and reduced germination
treatment: Treat seed with thiram, carbendazim, or iprodione

diseases.name: Blast (leaf, neck, nodal and collar)
diseases.description: A major fungal disease affecting leaves, nodes, collars, and panicle necks, often causing whiteheads.
symptoms: Diamond-shaped leaf lesions with gray centers and brown to reddish borders
Neck infection causes panicle girdling and whitehead
Dark lesions on nodes and rotting at leaf collar
treatment: Apply tricyclazole, isoprothiolane, or azoxystrobin at booting and heading
Remove volunteer rice and weed hosts

diseases.name: Brown spot
diseases.description: A common foliar and grain disease with brown lesions and potential severe leaf blighting.
symptoms: Circular to oval brown spots with dark margins and gray to whitish centers
Spots can coalesce and kill leaves
Dark discoloration on glumes and kernels
treatment: Treat seeds with thiram or captan
Avoid water stress and nutrient imbalance

diseases.name: Crown sheath rot
diseases.description: A crown and lower sheath rot associated with root blackening and premature plant death.
symptoms: Dark rotting lesions at crown and lower sheaths
Blackening of roots
Plants yellow, die early, and pull out easily

diseases.name: Downy mildew
diseases.description: An oomycete disease causing leaf distortion, downy growth, and malformed sterile panicles.
symptoms: Yellowing and distortion of leaves
White to grayish downy growth on lower leaf surface
Excess tillering with malformed sterile panicles
treatment: Improve drainage and reduce standing water

diseases.name: Eyespot
diseases.description: A foliar disease with pale-centered concentric lesions that resemble an eye.
symptoms: Oval to circular spots with pale centers and concentric brown rings
Eye-like lesions on leaf blades and sheaths
Progressive spotting under favorable conditions

diseases.name: False smut
diseases.description: A grain disease where kernels are replaced by orange to green-black velvety smut balls.
symptoms: Individual grains replaced by large velvety spore balls
Smut balls turn from orange-yellow to greenish-black
Only a few grains per panicle are usually affected
treatment: Apply propiconazole, tebuconazole, or copper oxychloride at booting
Collect and destroy smut balls before rupture

diseases.name: Kernel smut
diseases.description: A smut disease where mature grains are filled with black powdery teliospores.
symptoms: Glumes gape open at maturity
Partially or fully smutted grains contain black powder
Distinctive foul odor
treatment: Treat seed with carboxin, thiram, or tebuconazole
Apply propiconazole or hexaconazole at booting

diseases.name: Leaf smut
diseases.description: A generally minor disease producing dark angular sori on both leaf surfaces.
symptoms: Small angular black to dark olive sori on both leaf surfaces
Surrounding tissue may remain green
Heavy infection can cause yellowing and leaf death
treatment: Apply mancozeb or copper fungicide if severe

diseases.name: Leaf scald
diseases.description: A zonate leaf disease starting at tips and margins with concentric banding.
symptoms: Light brown to tan irregular lesions from leaf tips or margins
Onion-ring pattern with alternating dark and light bands
Severe infection causes complete leaf die-back
treatment: Reduce plant density and remove infected debris

diseases.name: Narrow brown leaf spot
diseases.pathogen: Cercospora janseana / Cercospora oryzae / Sphaerulina oryzina
diseases.description: A Cercospora disease with narrow vein-parallel brown streaks and premature leaf death.
symptoms: Very narrow linear brown streaks parallel to veins
Lesions are usually less than 2 mm wide
Heavy infection causes leaf yellowing and early death
treatment: Treat seeds with thiram and remove infected debris

diseases.name: Pecky rice (kernel spotting)
diseases.pathogen: Multiple fungi including Cochliobolus, Curvularia, Fusarium, Microdochium, and Sarocladium species
diseases.description: A complex grain spotting problem caused by multiple fungi, reducing milling quality.
symptoms: Brown, black, or pink spots on pericarp or endosperm
Chalky, empty, or shriveled kernels
Reduced grain quality and marketability
treatment: Apply iprodione or propiconazole during grain fill
Manage insects that create grain entry wounds

diseases.name: Root rots
diseases.description: Root decay complex causing poor tillering, stunting, and weak anchorage.
symptoms: Browning and decay of roots
Yellowing and stunting of shoots
Poor tillering and easy plant uprooting
treatment: Use metalaxyl for Pythium and carbendazim for Fusarium
Avoid transplanting into cold waterlogged soils

diseases.name: Rust
diseases.pathogen: Not applicable (rice is considered immune to rusts)
diseases.description: Rust is not considered a true disease concern in rice due to natural immunity.
symptoms: No rust symptoms in rice under normal field conditions

diseases.name: Seedling blight
diseases.pathogen: Cochliobolus, Curvularia, Fusarium, Rhizoctonia, and other fungi
diseases.description: A damping-off disease complex causing poor emergence and early seedling death.
symptoms: Pre- or post-emergence damping-off
Yellowing, wilting, and death of seedlings
Rotting of seed, coleoptile, or hypocotyl
treatment: Treat seed with thiram, carbendazim, or captan
Apply validamycin or iprodione drench in nursery

diseases.name: Sheath blight
diseases.description: A major sheath disease with waterline lesions, upward canopy spread, and lodging risk.
symptoms: Greenish-gray sheath lesions with wavy brown margins near waterline
Lesions spread upward to leaves and canopy
White mycelium and brown sclerotia on infected tissue
treatment: Apply validamycin, hexaconazole, propiconazole, or azoxystrobin

diseases.name: Sheath rot
diseases.description: A booting-stage sheath infection causing enclosed panicle sterility and chaffy grains.
symptoms: Irregular light brown sheath lesions with dark margins
Whitish to pinkish fungal growth inside sheath
Partially or fully sterile enclosed panicles
treatment: Apply carbendazim, propiconazole, or iprodione at booting

diseases.name: Sheath spot
diseases.description: A sheath disease with tan lesions and reddish-brown borders, often milder than sheath blight.
symptoms: Oval to elongated lesions on sheath
Tan to light brown centers with reddish-brown borders
Lesions can coalesce but are usually less severe than sheath blight

diseases.name: Stackburn (Alternaria leaf spot)
diseases.description: An Alternaria disease causing leaf and grain spotting with poor seed viability.
symptoms: Brown to dark brown oval or circular spots with yellow halos
Spots can occur on leaves, glumes, and grains
Infected seed may fail to germinate or produce weak seedlings
treatment: Treat seeds with thiram, captan, or iprodione
Dry stored grain below 13 percent moisture

diseases.name: Stem rot
diseases.description: A lower stem disease with sclerotia formation that causes culm collapse and yield loss.
symptoms: Small irregular black lesions on outer sheath near waterline
Rotting of inner stem tissue and culm collapse
Deadheart or premature heading with empty grains
treatment: Apply hexaconazole, propiconazole, or carbendazim at early tillering
Plow deep after harvest to bury sclerotia
Maintain balanced potassium and silicon nutrition

diseases.name: Water-mold (seed-rot and seedling disease)
diseases.pathogen: Achlya spp. / Fusarium spp. / Pythium spp.
diseases.description: A seed and seedling disease complex favored by cold, waterlogged nursery conditions.
symptoms: Pre-germination seed decay
Post-germination damping-off with yellowing and wilting
Cottony mycelial growth on seed or seedling base under water
treatment: Treat seed with metalaxyl for Pythium and Achlya
Use thiram or carbendazim for Fusarium
Avoid seeding into cold waterlogged soils
